title: Can Eva return to feeling
wikitext: []: [SPOILER] Can Eva return to 'feeling'?             Quote Post by Boullée » Thu May 02, 2013 4:11 pm Firstly, I predict that in the course of writing the next few paragraphs I will be using some pretty ambiguous terminology dealing with things like ‘feeling’ and ‘meaning’; and might come off as excessively emo. So be it. I watched 3.33, I was disappointed, I watched it again and was disappointed slightly less. Then thought: what is making me enjoy it that little bit more?The realisation came that it was probably observing more references and details and having read some of the speculation and analysis. It turns out that what made the subsequent viewing more digestible was having things to geek out over, pausing the video and inspecting frames. And yet it occurs to me that that this is not what I loved Evangelion for. Nope, like manypeople , Evangelion somehow changed life . You see, what was great about Evangelion was not that it depicted an intricately built universe (plot) that needed merchandise, video games and books to explain it, but that it depicted something something awfully ‘real’ (story). I make a distinction between the plot (things that happen) and story (what and how those things communicate). The current rebuild films seem to be full of plot to geek out over for the next few months, but very few human, ponderous, moments of story that make one feel. It is reasonably well documented how NGE came off the back of Anno’s depressive period, which comes as no surprise to anybody who has watched the show. Gainax was going through some tough times as well, and the budget restrictions lead to some rather unique storytelling. I don’t have insight into Anno’s personal life or Khara’s budget, but it seems that the situation is vastly different than all those years ago. The question is then, is there any desire by Anno and the studio to even make something of the same emotional calibre as the original iteration? It acknowledged that NGE contained elements ofanti-otakuism , as well as much of Anno’s selfreflection , yet we know that Anno is capable of geeking out as much as the best ofus . It makes me wonder if we can expect something as deeply personal as NGE from Rebuild. Well, perhaps doubt would be the better term, as the current direction of Rebuild is steering towards flat characters instead of meaningful interactions. Of course, it is likely that Khara knows exactly what it’s doing, and does not mind. Perhaps they want to make the most convoluted plot of all time, or perhaps they want to make it seem that way just to be able to ignore it in the last one and show the plot as meaningless. Perhaps. My question is, then, do you think that Eva 4.0 can, or will, steer closer to telling the emotional development of Shinji, or instead clear the air of the plot? And if it chooses the story, is it too little too late? There is one further thing that comes to mind: This is roughly the world-view for Neon Genesis Evangelion. This is a wore view drenched in a vision of pessimism. A world-view where the story starts only after any traces of optimism have been removed. - Hideaki Anno , 1995.

 out as much as the best ofus . It makes me wonder if we can expect something as deeply personal as NGE from Rebuild. Well, perhaps doubt would be the better term, as the current direction of Rebuild is steering towards flat characters instead of meaningful interactions. Of course, it is likely that Khara knows exactly what it’s doing, and does not mind. Perhaps they want to make the most convoluted plot of all time, or perhaps they want to make it seem that way just to be able to ignore it in the last one and show the plot as meaningless. Perhaps.
